stonetop.summary: Elemental spirits bound in aetherium matrices, manifesting as whirling vortices.

system.concept: Elemental spirits bound in aetherium matrices, manifesting as whirling vortices.
system.description: Picture a filigree sphere of aetherium, maybe six inches across, with a glowing mote swirling at its center. This device — a matrix — binds an elemental spirit and absorbs a particular force, with which the spirit manifests a swirling vortex. A bound spirit must obey orders given in the Tempest Lords' primordial tongue, but as almost none alive can speak it, any matrix encountered is likely acting on orders centuries old. A vortex's current HP reflects its current power: as it gains or loses HP, its size, armor, and damage all change — tiny (1-5 HP: Armor 1, d8-2), small (6-11: Armor 2, d8), medium (12-18: Armor 2, d10), large (19-23: Armor 3, d10+3), huge (24-28: Armor 3, d10+5). At 0 HP it dissipates and the inert matrix drops. The matrix itself has only 3 HP; destroy it and the freed spirit lashes out for the vortex's full damage (+area).
